description.en: Egypt's everyday bread: a whole wheat pocket flatbread with a wet, slack dough dusted in bran, blasted in a very hot oven until it balloons. Torn warm at every meal to scoop ful medamas, taameya or a dip; the word aish itself means life.
description.de: Ägyptens tägliches Brot: ein Vollkorn-Taschenfladen aus nassem, weichem Teig mit Kleie bestäubt, im sehr heißen Ofen aufgebläht. Warm zu jeder Mahlzeit gerissen, um Ful medammes, Taameya oder einen Dip aufzunehmen; aish bedeutet wörtlich Leben.
description.es: El pan diario de Egipto: un pan plano integral con bolsillo, de masa húmeda y floja espolvoreada con salvado, horneado a fuego muy fuerte hasta inflarse. Se rompe tibio en cada comida para recoger ful medames, taameya o alguna salsa; aish significa literalmente vida.
description.fr: Le pain quotidien de l'Égypte: un pain plat complet à poche, à la pâte humide et molle saupoudrée de son, cuit à four très chaud jusqu'à gonfler. On le déchire tiède à chaque repas pour saucer le ful medames, le taameya ou une sauce; aish signifie littéralement la vie.

origin.note: Aish means both 'bread' and 'life' in Egyptian Arabic; government-subsidized baladi bread is a politically sensitive staple, and price changes have historically triggered unrest.

ingredients.note: for dusting the work surface and coating the dough

keeping.note: Best warm the same day; once cooled it dries out quickly, so wrap airtight or freeze for later toasting.

method.title: Bloom yeast
method.summary: Dissolve sugar and yeast in warm water; let stand about 10 minutes until foamy.

method.title: Sponge
method.summary: Stir in half the flour and let the wet mixture proof for 30 minutes.

method.title: Mix and knead
method.summary: Work in the remaining flour, salt and oil; knead about 10 minutes to a soft, notably wet dough.

method.title: Rise
method.summary: Cover and let the dough double in size, about 1 hour.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Divide into 8 balls, roll each thin on a bran-dusted surface, and rest covered for 30 minutes.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake on a screaming-hot stone until puffed and lightly charred in spots, 6 to 7 minutes.

source.adaptation: Cup/tablespoon quantities converted to grams; wheat bran treated as a coating/dusting ingredient rather than folded into the flour total, matching its use on the work surface.
